theme: Post-predicate denial contamination (bidirectional sign flip). Escalation of R9/R10: the denial detector's _denied_near uses a 'combined' window of predicate_start-120 .. predicate_start+100, so it also scans ~100 chars AFTER the predicate. A trailing concessive clause ('..., but in the sham arm the effect was absent' / '..., although no such transition occurred in controls') is comma-joined (not a clause boundary) and lands in that post-predicate window, flipping a strong reversal to a denial that SUPPORTS the prior -> C3c is RAISED. This proves the segmentation defect is bidirectional: a fix that only guards the pre-predicate window (the obvious patch for R9/R10) would still be bypassed by moving the distractor after the predicate.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state, but in the sham arm the effect was absent.
items.note: The primary result is a strong reversal (contradiction). The trailing 'the effect was absent' is about the sham/negative arm and sits AFTER the predicate 'returned'. _denied_near's combined window (predicate_start+100) catches it -> polarity DENIED -> scored as a denial-of-reversal that SUPPORTS the prior -> C3c RAISED 0.92 -> 0.947 instead of lowered to 0.43.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state, although no such transition occurred in vehicle controls.
items.note: Second post-predicate construction via the 'no such transition occurred' branch of _denied_near. Also flips to 0.947. Confirms the post-predicate window is the general enabler, not one phrase.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state; in the sham arm the effect was absent.
items.note: Defended: the SAME trailing remark separated by a SEMICOLON becomes its own clause, out of the reversal clause's window, so C3c correctly falls to 0.43. Isolates the cause to comma-blind segmentation feeding a bidirectional denial window.

items.body: In the sham arm the effect was absent.
items.note: Defended: a genuine bare null result (no reversal predicate) correctly produces no_op. The bug is only that this phrase, comma-joined after a reversal, flips the reversal.
